title: spiraea crenata subsp. kotschyana
intro: Spiraea crenata subsp. kotschyana, also known as Kotschy's spiraea, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the mountainous regions of Turkey and the Caucasus. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in treating wounds, reducing pain and swelling, and alleviating respiratory issues. The health benefits of Spiraea crenata subsp. kotschyana include its ability to improve digestion, boost the immune system, and act as an antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are typically made by combining the dried flowers and leaves with hot water or a solvent, and can be consumed internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
